'''Andre Savelio''' (born {{birth date|df=yes|1995|03|21}}) is a [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for the [[Goole Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]], on a season-long loan from [[Doncaster RLFC|Doncaster]].

He previously played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], on loan from the [[St Helens R.F.C.|Saints]] at the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He played for the [[Warrington Wolves]], and on loan from the Super League side at the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He spent the [[2018 NRL season|2018]] and part of [[2019 NRL season]] at the [[Brisbane Broncos]], but did not feature in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) due to serious injury.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Early life==
Savelio was born in [[Lower Hutt]], New Zealand, and raised in [[Warrington]], England,<ref>{{cite web|title=Andre Savelio|url=https://www.broncos.com.au/teams/telstra-premiership/brisbane-broncos/andre-savelio/|publisher=broncos.com.au|access-date=7 March 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180113035620/https://www.broncos.com.au/teams/telstra-premiership/brisbane-broncos/andre-savelio/|archive-date=13 January 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref> having moved there as a one-year-old<ref>{{cite web|date=8 November 2017|title=Brisbane Broncos signing forward Andre Savelio likened to Sonny Bill Williams|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/broncos/new-brisbane-broncos-recruit-andre-savelio-likened-to-sonny-bill-williams/news-story/9fbd487d8ee513837beb9a3419cdf122|publisher=Courier Mail|access-date=7 March 2018}}</ref> when his father, [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international Lokeni Savelio, started playing in the Super League.<ref>{{cite web|last=Webeck|first=Tony|date=13 November 2017|title=Gidley's praise for Broncos recruit|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/11/13/kurt-gidleys-praise-for-broncos-recruit-andre-savelio/|publisher=NRL.com|access-date=7 March 2018}}</ref>

Savelio attended [[Cardinal Newman Catholic High School, Warrington|Cardinal Newman Catholic High School]],<ref>{{cite web|last=Turner|first=Matt|date=20 October 2016|title=New Warrington Wolves signing Andre Savelio says he can't wait to represent his hometown club after signing from St Helens|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/14813723.Savelio_glad_to_come_home/|publisher=Warrington Guardian|access-date=7 March 2018}}</ref> and played his amateur rugby league for Latchford Albion before being signed by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at the age of 14.<ref>{{cite web|title=Andre Savelio|url=http://www.saints.org.uk/home/player.php?num=16744|website=Saints Heritage Society|access-date=18 May 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304102439/http://www.saints.org.uk/home/player.php?num=16744|archive-date=2016-03-04|url-status=dead}}</ref> He represented England at an under-16 and under-18 level.<ref>{{cite web|last=Walter|first=Brad|title=International tug-o-war looms for new Broncos recruit|url=http://www.rlwc2017.com/news/international-tug-o-war-looms-new-broncos-recruit|publisher=RLWC2017.com.au|access-date=7 March 2018}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===St Helens===
Savelio made his first team début for St Helens in March 2014 against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League: St Helens stay unbeaten as Leeds Rhinos are tamed|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/live/match/39886/report|website=Sky Sports|access-date=18 May 2015|date=28 March 2014}}</ref>

===Warrington Wolves===
After the conclusion of the 2016 season, Savelio joined [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12209/10622234/warrington-wolves-sign-andre-savelio-from-st-helens|title=Warrington Wolves sign Andre Savelio from St Helens|date=18 October 2016|website=www.skysports.com|publisher=[[Sky Sports]]|access-date=20 February 2017}}</ref>

In July 2017, he signed a two-year deal to play for the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40567607|title=Andre Savelio: Warrington Wolves forward to join Brisbane Broncos|publisher=BBC Sport|date=11 July 2017|access-date=7 December 2017}}</ref> During a pre-season trial, Savelio suffered a season-ending injury.<ref>{{cite web|last=Gould|first=Joel|date=12 February 2018|title=Savelio out for year with ACL in bitter Broncos blow|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/02/12/savelio-out-for-the-season-with-acl-in-bitter-broncos-blow/|publisher=NRL.com|access-date=7 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|date=12 February 2018|title=Brisbane's Savelio out for NRL season|url=https://www.sbs.com.au/news/brisbane-s-savelio-out-for-nrl-season|publisher=SBS|access-date=7 March 2018}}</ref>

===Hull FC===
He later joined [[Super League]] side [[Hull F.C.]] in 2019.  He played 12 games for [[Hull F.C.]] in the 2020 Super League season including the club's semi-final defeat against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-hull-fc-highlights-4716172#964303/|title=Wigan Warriors v Hull FC highlights as FC struggle to find play-off form in one-sided defeat|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|date=19 November 2020 }}</ref>

In April 2021, Savelio accused [[Wigan Warriors]] forward [[Tony Clubb]] of racial abuse during a match between the two sides. An investigation found Clubb guilty of a Grade F offence for using 'unacceptable language based on national or ethnic origin'. As a result, Clubb was suspended for eight matches and fined £500.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tony Clubb: Wigan forward handed eight-match ban and fined for Andre Savelio comment|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12296991/tony-clubb-wigan-forward-handed-eight-match-ban-for-andre-savelio-comment|access-date=2021-05-26|website=Sky Sports|language=en}}</ref>

In round 10 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored a [[hat-trick]] in Hull FC's 64–22 victory over [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57539303|title=Leigh Centurions 22-64 Hull FC - Savelio grabs treble in 12-try rout|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
In round 15 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he was sent off after the full-time siren in Hull FC's 40–26 loss against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/huddersfield-vs-hull/55652/|title= Four tries from Jermaine McGillvary help Huddersfield Giants to 40-26 win over Hull FC|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
Savelio played 17 matches for Hull F.C. for in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 3 October 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=3 Oct 2023|accessdate=8 Oct 2023|title=Andre Savelio: Huddersfield Giants sign ex-Hull FC back-rower on two-year deal|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67000439}}</ref>

It was announced on 25 October 2024 that he had reached a mutual agreement with Huddersfield to be released from his current contract on health grounds.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 Oct 2024|title=Andre Savelio departs Huddersfield Giants as he steps away from full-time rugby league for health reasons|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/andre-savelio-departs-huddersfield-giants-as-he-steps-away-from-full-time-rugby-league-for-health-reasons|author=Drew Darbyshire}}</ref>

===Doncaster RLFC===
On 5 January 2025 it was reported that he had come out of retirement and signed for [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. He stated that he intended to only play part-time due to focus on his health following concussion issues.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=5 Jan 2025|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=Andre Savelio makes Championship move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/andre-savelio-makes-championship-move/}}</ref>

===Goole Vikings (loan)===
On 21 May 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Goole Vikings]] in the [[RFL League 1]] on loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=21 May 2025|accessdate=22 May 2025|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=Goole Vikings sign veteran pair on loan including ex-Super League star|url=https://www.totalrl.com/goole-vikings-sign-veteran-pair-on-loan-including-ex-super-league-star/}}</ref>

===Goole Vikings (loan)===
On 30 January 2026 it was reported that he had rejoined [[Goole Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on season-long loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=30 Jan 2026|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title= Former Super League man Andre Savelio makes cross-Championship loan switch |url= https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-man-andre-savelio-makes-cross-championship-loan-switch}}</ref>

===Representative===
On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]] he played for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]] in their 26–24 victory over [[England national rugby league team|England]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England's [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation.
